project_type: C++17 AI operating system / hybrid reasoning engine
build_system: Makefile with clang++ direct compilation. The unified binary is built by compiling system/mk_entry.cpp which #includes all other .cpp files. No separate object file compilation for the main binary.
test_framework: None currently. No test infrastructure exists.
verification_instructions: 1. Run the build command and verify exit code 0 with no errors (warnings are ok). 2. After tests are added, compile and run the test binary.
setup_instructions: No setup needed. clang++, libcurl, and libsqlite3 are available in the sandbox.
environment_constraints: Open internet access. Linux sandbox (not macOS, so -DDARWIN is set but some macOS-specific system calls in diagnostics.cpp won't work at runtime). Build verification via compilation only.
contribution_requirements: No CONTRIBUTING.md. The project uses a single-binary include-based architecture where mk_entry.cpp #includes all module .cpp files. New modules must be added as #include directives in mk_entry.cpp.
key_patterns: 1. All modules use #ifndef guards and are designed to be #included. 2. Classes are defined in .cpp files (not .h files). 3. The MKSystem struct in mk_entry.cpp holds instances of all wired modules. 4. Dead main() files exist in main_system.cpp, main_plugins.cpp, main_network.cpp, ai_core/main.cpp, scheduler/main_scheduler.cpp, Kernel/kernel_main.cpp, ai_core/hre/hre_main.cpp. 5. The Makefile excludes these dead mains via ! -path and ! -name patterns but the real build just compiles mk_entry.cpp directly.
directory_structure: MK_OS/ is the root. system/ has the entry point and system services. ai_core/ has AI modules and ai_core/hre/ has the hybrid reasoning engine with knowledge_files/. mk_brain/ has learning, memory, embeddings, vector search, cache, fact extraction, reasoning. network/ has HTTP and search. plugins/ has telegram and helpers. Kernel/ is a separate simulated kernel. tools/ has standalone tool binaries.
